findings.evidence: `rg` shows the existing API at `WeilDivisor/AbelJacobi.lean:52: noncomputable def weightedAbelJacobiClass ...`; the new def is `BasepointChange.lean:52-54: noncomputable def weightedBasepointChangeClass ... := S.weightedAbelJacobiClass w hdeg hy₀ x₀`, with rfl equivalences at lines 105-117.
findings.fix: Remove `weightedBasepointChangeClass` and state the change-base lemmas directly with `S.weightedAbelJacobiClass w hdeg hy₀ x₀` as the translation term.
findings.issue: `weightedBasepointChangeClass` is definitionally just `S.weightedAbelJacobiClass w hdeg hy₀ x₀`, so the new declaration and its rfl bridge lemmas duplicate the existing weighted Abel-Jacobi class API.

findings.evidence: `rg` shows `WeilDivisor/AbelJacobi.lean:99-101: noncomputable def unweightedAbelJacobiClass ... := S.weightedAbelJacobiClass ...`; the PR adds `BasepointChange.lean:133-135: noncomputable def unweightedBasepointChangeClass ... := S.weightedBasepointChangeClass ...`, plus the rfl alias `unweightedAbelJacobiClass_eq_basepointChangeClass` at lines 187-191.
findings.fix: Remove `unweightedBasepointChangeClass` and write the unweighted formulas using `S.unweightedAbelJacobiClass hdeg y₀ x₀` directly.
findings.issue: `unweightedBasepointChangeClass` repeats the same duplication in the unweighted specialization; it is just the existing `S.unweightedAbelJacobiClass hdeg y₀ x₀` behind the weighted synonym.

summary: The new basepoint-change class API is a definitional duplicate of the existing Abel-Jacobi class API, creating a parallel name for the same object instead of reusing it.
